channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [IEX*] IEX FY26 Consolidated PAT up 15% to
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Indian Energy Exchange (IEX) reported robust financial results for the fiscal year and fourth quarter ending March 31, 2026. For FY26, the company achieved its highest ever traded electricity volume of 141 BU, marking a 17% year-on-year increase. Consolidated Profit After Tax (PAT) for the year stood at ₹492.9 crore, up 15% from the previous year. Standalone revenue was ₹744.9 crore (up 13.9%) and standalone PAT was ₹473.7 crore (up 14.2%). The Board of Directors declared a final dividend of ₹2 per equity share. In Q4FY26, IEX recorded its highest ever quarterly traded electricity volume of 39.4 BU, a 24% increase YoY, with consolidated PAT at ₹129.8 crore, up 11% YoY. The gas exchange (IGX) also saw significant growth, trading 76.8 million MMBtu in FY26, up 28% YoY, and reporting a PAT of ₹41.9 crore, a 35% increase.

  • FY26 consolidated PAT increased by 15% to ₹492.9 crore.
  • Achieved highest ever traded electricity volume of 141 BU in FY26, up 17% YoY.
  • Board recommended a final dividend of ₹2 per equity share (200%).
  • Q4FY26 saw highest ever quarterly electricity volume of 39.4 BU, up 24% YoY.
  • Q4FY26 consolidated PAT grew by 11% to ₹129.8 crore.
  • IGX gas volumes grew 28% YoY, with PAT up 35% to ₹41.9 crore.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Indian Energy Exchange Ltd | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [PAEL] PAE Ltd Board Approves FY26 Results, Recommends Dividend & 6:1 Bonus
item.description:
🔥 Trending

PAE Ltd's Board of Directors, in a meeting held on April 23, 2026, approved the audited standalone financial results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026. The board recommended a final dividend of ₹0.20 per equity share (face value ₹10) for the financial year 2025-26. The 76th Annual General Meeting (AGM) is scheduled for May 18, 2026, with May 11, 2026, fixed as the record date for the dividend. Additionally, the board recommended a bonus issue of equity shares in the proportion of 6:1 (six bonus shares for every one existing share) to public shareholders (excluding Promoter and Promoter Group) to ensure compliance with minimum public shareholding requirements. May 25, 2026, has been set as the record date for the bonus issue. The board also approved an addition to the Objects of the Company and a subsequent alteration of the Memorandum of Association, subject to shareholder approval.

  • Board approved audited standalone financial results for FY26.
  • Recommended final dividend of ₹0.20 per equity share for FY26.
  • Proposed 6:1 bonus issue for public shareholders to meet regulations.
  • 76th AGM scheduled for May 18, 2026, with dividend record date May 11.
  • Approved alteration of Memorandum of Association, subject to shareholder nod.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: PAE LTD. | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [INFY*] Infosys Recommends ₹25/Share Final Dividend; Record Date June 10, 2026
item.description:
Infosys Ltd's Board of Directors, at their meeting on April 22-23, 2026, approved the audited financial results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026. The Board recommended a final dividend of ₹25/- per equity share for the financial year ended March 31, 2026. The record date for this dividend payment is set for June 10, 2026, with the actual payment scheduled for June 25, 2026. Additionally, the company announced its 45th Annual General Meeting for June 23, 2026. The Board also approved annual performance-based stock incentives for CEO & MD Salil Parekh totaling ₹51.75 crore, and other employee stock grants including 27,193 RSUs and PSUs worth ₹1.90 crore. Furthermore, BSR & Co. LLP has been proposed as the new statutory auditors due to mandatory rotation requirements.

  • Board recommended a final dividend of ₹25 per equity share for FY26.
  • Dividend record date is June 10, 2026, with payment on June 25, 2026.
  • Approved ₹51.75 crore in stock grants for CEO & MD, and other employee grants.
  • Proposed BSR & Co. LLP as new statutory auditors due to mandatory rotation.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: INFOSYS LTD. | Category: Dividend

item.title: [GUJHOTE] Gujarat Hotels approves FY26 results; recommends ₹3.00 dividend
item.description:
Gujarat Hotels Ltd's Board of Directors, at a meeting on April 23, 2026, approved the audited financial results for the quarter and twelve months ended March 31, 2026. The company reported a Profit for the Period of ₹565.65 lakhs for FY26. The statutory auditors, K C Mehta & Co. LLP, provided an unmodified opinion on these results. Additionally, the Board recommended a final dividend of ₹3.00 per equity share for the financial year ended March 31, 2026, subject to shareholder approval at the forthcoming Annual General Meeting.

  • Board approved audited financial results for Q4 and FY26.
  • FY26 Profit for the Period stood at ₹565.65 lakhs.
  • Statutory Auditors issued an unmodified opinion on the results.
  • Recommended final dividend of ₹3.00 per equity share for FY26.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: GUJARAT HOTELS LTD. | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [BJDUP] BJ Duplex Boards: EGM Corrigendum for Name Change Certificate
item.description:
BJ Duplex Boards Ltd has issued a corrigendum to its Extraordinary General Meeting (EGM) notice, addressing an inadvertent omission. The company has now annexed the required Practicing Chartered Accountant's certificate, as per Regulation 45 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015. This certificate, crucial for the proposed name change, confirms that the company has not changed its name in the last year and that 100% of its ₹8.50 Lakhs revenue in the preceding year is derived from the new activity. The certificate now forms an integral part of the EGM Notice.

  • Corrigendum issued for EGM notice regarding a proposed name change.
  • Previously omitted CA certificate for name change compliance now included.
  • Certificate confirms 100% of ₹8.50 Lakhs revenue from new activity.
  • Ensures compliance with SEBI Regulation 45 for the name change process.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: B.J.DUPLEX BOARDS LTD. | Category: Clarification

item.title: [MAHLOG] MLL FY26 Revenue up 15% to ₹6,999 Cr; Returns to Profitability with PAT ₹2.3 Cr
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Mahindra Logistics Ltd. (MLL) announced its audited consolidated financial results for FY26, reporting a 15% year-on-year revenue increase to ₹6,999 crores. The company returned to profitability with a reported PAT of ₹2.3 crores for FY26, compared to a loss in the previous year. For Q4 FY26, consolidated revenue grew to ₹1,791 crores from ₹1,570 crores in Q4 FY25, and reported PAT was ₹20.2 crores, a significant improvement from a loss of ₹6.7 crores in the prior year. Key business segments like Express, Freight Forwarding, and Mobility demonstrated strong growth and improved profitability, with Express Business achieving a positive gross margin for FY26.

  • FY26 consolidated revenue grew 15% YoY to ₹6,999 crores.
  • Company returned to profitability in FY26 with reported PAT of ₹2.3 crores.
  • Q4 FY26 consolidated revenue increased to ₹1,791 crores, PAT at ₹20.2 crores.
  • Express Business revenue grew 25% YoY, achieving positive gross margin in FY26.
  • Freight Forwarding revenue rose 14% YoY, with EBITDA up 48%.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Mahindra Logistics Ltd | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [HINDCOMPOS] Hindustan Composites Board Recommends ₹2 Dividend for FY26
item.description:
The Board of Directors of Hindustan Composites Ltd, at its meeting on April 23, 2026, approved the audited standalone and consolidated financial results for the quarter and financial year ended March 31, 2026. The Board recommended a final dividend of ₹2 per equity share for FY 2025-26, subject to shareholder approval at the upcoming Annual General Meeting. Additionally, the Board re-appointed M/s. Suresh Pimple & Associates as Cost Auditors for FY 2026-27 and approved a capacity expansion plan for the same financial year. The company also clarified its position on a past fine for delayed financial results submission, stating it was not in default and has applied for a waiver.

  • Board approved audited Q4 and FY26 standalone and consolidated financial results.
  • Recommended a final dividend of ₹2 per equity share for FY 2025-26.
  • Re-appointed M/s. Suresh Pimple & Associates as Cost Auditors for FY 2026-27.
  • Approved capacity expansion plan for the financial year 2026-27.
  • Clarified position on a past fine for delayed results, seeking waiver from BSE.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: HINDUSTAN COMPOSITES LTD. | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [STARCEMENT] Star Cement subsidiary acquires 100% of Jaitaran Renewable Power
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Star Cement North East Limited (SCNEL), a subsidiary of Star Cement Ltd, has acquired 100% shareholding of Jaitaran Renewable Power Private Limited (JRPPL) for a cash consideration of ₹20,000. Consequently, JRPPL has become a wholly-owned subsidiary of SCNEL and a step-down subsidiary of Star Cement Limited. JRPPL, incorporated on March 11, 2026, operates in the Energy & Power industry. The acquisition aims to diversify Star Cement's business into a new vertical and strengthen its initiatives towards renewable energy. The transaction is not a related party transaction. JRPPL has an authorized share capital of ₹10.00 Lakhs and a paid-up capital of ₹0.20 Lakhs, with nil turnover for 2025.

  • Star Cement's subsidiary acquired 100% of Jaitaran Renewable Power.
  • The acquisition diversifies business into the renewable energy sector.
  • JRPPL becomes a wholly-owned step-down subsidiary.
  • Acquisition cost was ₹20,000 for 100% stake.
  • JRPPL is a newly incorporated entity in Energy & Power.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Star Cement Ltd | Category: Acquisitions

item.title: [NETLINK] Netlink Solutions Appoints 2, Sees 2 Independent Directors Resign
item.description:
Netlink Solutions India Ltd. announced significant changes to its Board of Directors. The company appointed Ms. Aanal Satyawadi and Ms. Yesha Shah as Additional Non-Executive Independent Directors, effective April 23, 2026. These appointments are subject to shareholder approval at the ensuing General Meeting. Concurrently, Mr. Manoj Ashok Pardhee and Mr. Pankaj Vrajlal Sodha resigned from their positions as Independent Directors, effective from the close of business hours on April 23, 2026. Their resignations were attributed to a change in management and control following the completion of an open offer under SEBI regulations. Consequently, the outgoing directors also ceased to be members and chairpersons of all Board Committees. Ms. Aanal Satyawadi is a qualified Company Secretary and legal professional with over 10 years of experience in corporate laws and regulatory advisory.

  • Two new independent directors, Ms. Aanal Satyawadi and Ms. Yesha Shah, appointed.
  • Two independent directors, Mr. Manoj Pardhee and Mr. Pankaj Sodha, resigned.
  • Resignations are due to change in management and control post open offer.
  • New appointments are effective April 23, 2026, subject to shareholder approval.
  • Ms. Satyawadi brings over 10 years of legal and secretarial experience.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: NETLINK SOLUTIONS (INDIA) LTD. | Category: Management Change

item.title: [NETLINK] Netlink Solutions Appoints 2, Sees 2 Independent Directors Resign
item.description:
Netlink Solutions India Ltd. announced significant changes to its Board of Directors. The Board approved the appointment of Ms. Aanal Satyawadi (DIN: 07381381) and Ms. Yesha Shah (DIN: 08802522) as Additional Non-Executive Independent Directors, effective April 23, 2026. These appointments are subject to shareholder approval at the ensuing General Meeting. Concurrently, Mr. Manoj Ashok Pardhee (DIN: 10744070) and Mr. Pankaj Vrajlal Sodha (DIN: 10744650) resigned from their positions as Independent Directors, effective from the close of business hours on April 23, 2026. Their resignations are attributed to the change in management and control following the completion of an open offer. Consequently, they also ceased to be members and chairpersons of all Board Committees. Ms. Satyawadi brings over 10 years of experience in SEBI compliances, FEMA, RBI matters, and corporate laws as a Practicing Company Secretary.

  • Board approved appointment of two new Independent Directors.
  • Ms. Aanal Satyawadi and Ms. Yesha Shah join from April 23, 2026.
  • Two Independent Directors, Mr. Pardhee and Mr. Sodha, resigned.
  • Resignations linked to change in management post open offer.
  • New appointments are subject to shareholder approval.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: NETLINK SOLUTIONS (INDIA) LTD. | Category: Management Change
